mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the supply chain, leading to higher demand for equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in pre-recession periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in the supply chain, leading to higher demand for equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in consumer demand, leading to higher equities prices and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in initial unemployment claims signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in pre-recession periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 3-month increase in the CPI signal an easing monetary cycle and a decrease in inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production and a 3-month increase in housing starts signal an expansion in the supply chain, leading to higher demand for equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
